title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Macro/Geopolitics,Commodity Markets,Freight Markets
As widely expected, the US economy bounced back in the third quarter following a contraction in the preceding three months. And relief rallies of this magnitude are surprisingly good
Yet, the festive and perma-happy crowd seems to disagree with me, almost as much as equity markets. Read More

Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
Τhe first days of November are marked by heightened geopolitical uncertainty after the Russian Federation suspended its participation in the Black Sea Grains Initiative.

children.summary: Much of today's focus will be on the Federal Reserve and its decision on interest rates.

children.title: Picking a Side
children.summary:
With just over 5 weeks to go until the European Union's ban on Russian crude oil imports come into effect, companies are now being forced to decide which market they wish to trade in. Read More

children.title: It remains encouraging that China's steel output has remained well above last year's level
section_path: It remains encouraging that China's steel output has remained well above last year's level
children.section_path_text: It remains encouraging that China's steel output has remained well above last year's level
children.summary:
Recently released data shows that daily crude output at large and medium-sized mills in China averaged 2.06 million tons during October 11 - 20. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-guADzpF9pDI.jpg | assets/2022-11-06_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-guadzpf9pdi_8fbc8c28a887.jpg

children.title: Mixed signs
children.summary:
Commodity markets were mixed as signs of tightness via lower inventories was weighed against the gloomy economic backdrop. Read More
Image reference: unsplash-image-J27FZUC7SMc.jpg | assets/2022-11-06_weekly-insights-review_img_unsplash-image-j27fzuc7smc_917d3e9e93c5.jpg

children.title: Weaker Saudi crude loadings constrained total OPEC+ seaborne supply in October, despite a m-o-m increase in loadings from Africa and other OPEC Middle East producers
section_path: Weaker Saudi crude loadings constrained total OPEC+ seaborne supply in October, despite a m-o-m increase in loadings from Africa and other OPEC Middle East producers
children.section_path_text: Weaker Saudi crude loadings constrained total OPEC+ seaborne supply in October, despite a m-o-m increase in loadings from Africa and other OPEC Middle East producers
children.summary: Total OPEC+ crude/condensate loadings in October stood at around 29.5, of which OPEC Middle East's contributed 18.5mbd - both totals show little change from September.
